name: Node Health Readonly Triage
summary: Collect read-only Slurm node evidence for support triage.
description: This skill helps HPC support teams collect user-visible Slurm node, queue, and recent accounting evidence so they can distinguish job-level failures from possible node or partition incidents without changing scheduler state.

tools.name: scontrol
tools.notes: Some sites restrict node details to support staff.

tools.name: sacct
tools.notes: Useful when accounting visibility includes recent jobs on a node.

inputs.name: node_name
inputs.description: Optional Slurm node name to inspect.

inputs.name: days
inputs.description: Number of recent days to include in optional accounting reports.

inputs.name: output_dir
inputs.description: Directory where the triage report should be written.

outputs.name: node_triage_report
outputs.description: A local directory containing read-only node state, queue, and recent accounting snapshots.

outputs.name: summary
outputs.description: A summary file describing scope, generated files, and safe handoff guidance.

tests.description: Validate the manifest and report script reference.
Check shell syntax without contacting a scheduler.
Run on a Slurm site with a known node name and confirm the report contains only approved operational details before sharing.
